findings.problem: Several pressure-heavy exchanges slide from sharpness into stylized telegraphy or over-formality. Mina and Tomas are high-contraction speakers in the style bible, yet the chapter gives them clipped question forms and Rhea gets repeated full-form legalistic phrasing; the result reads more like authored terseness than three distinct people talking under stress.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework the spoken beats anchored at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:15, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:17, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:47, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:53, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:189, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:279 so Mina and Tomas default to natural colloquial pressure speech and Rhea stays exact without sounding procedurally scripted. Preserve the scene's bite, but swap telegraphic word order and unnecessary full forms for contractions, interruptions, or sharper local phrasing that matches each profile.
findings.acceptance_test: Pass if a read-through of the exchanges anchored at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:15, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:47, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:53, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:189, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:279 lets a reader hear Mina/Tomas as high-contraction speakers and Rhea as a moderate-contraction exact speaker without any line relying on clipped 'question-reordered' syntax or repeated uncontracted legalese unless the scene itself marks that register as deliberate.

findings.problem: The chapter leans too often on the same negation-and-correction sentence shape and then restates Mina's non-absolution insight in the close. What starts as controlled rhythm turns into an audible tic, and the final pages explain the meaning after the scene has already earned it.
findings.rewrite_direction: Recast the repeated negation ladders and closing paraphrase around snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:133, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:139, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:311, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:383, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:385. Keep Mina's realization, but vary the sentence architecture and let the deletion, return ride, and dawn walk carry more of the chapter's meaning instead of restating abstraction in successive beats.
findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the final movement anchored at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:293, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:311, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:371, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:383 delivers Mina's changed relation to the eel primarily through concrete image/action, with no repeated stack of negation-ladder constructions and no second abstract restatement of 'not absolution' after the closing image has landed.

summary: Chapter 17 satisfies the vigil-engine requirements, preserves continuity, and renders the spawning discovery with real ecological and emotional consequence. It falls short of pass level on dialogue and prose because some spoken lines go stiff or telegraphic against the style-bible registers, and the closing pages repeat a negation-heavy thematic template instead of trusting the scene's already-earned force.
